ksa_text: Knowledge of global data protection regulations and privacy laws, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, PDPA, and sector-specific privacy obligations, with focus on applicable legal bases for processing and regulatory enforcement.

eu_ai_act.top_element_label: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA SP-K-002 (Security and Privacy/Knowledge) scores 0.444 composite (highest: O*NET at 0.444) and is edge_case for the AI Product Manager role. Domain prior: edge. Core signal hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority is appropriate for manager-level AI Product Manager.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, O*NET signal (0.444), and Security and Privacy handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support Security/Privacy competencies for AI Product Manager?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Privacy and data security competencies appear in 25-60% of AI Product Manager postings, especially in regulated industries (finance, healthcare, public sector).
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. Security content is policy/org-level, not data implementation. Not market-supported. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. Security content is policy/org-level, not data implementation. Not market-supported. Final decision: exclude.
